title: thymus serpyllum subsp. praecox
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. praecox, commonly known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe, with tiny oval leaves and pink or white flowers. It is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote healthy digestion. The herb can be prepared in various ways, including making tea from the leaves, using it in potpourri or sachets for aromatherapy, and incorporating it into salves and ointments for topical application.
